title: Week 28
summary:
Capesize
A positive end to the week for capesizes as rates recovered after an earlier short lived dip in rates. Rates steadied and showed signs of moving up on key routes. The West Australia/China route appeared to closing out around $5.60 for 25 July onwards for 170,000 tonnes 10% steady though had reached $5.70 a couple of days ago.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A positive end to the week for capesizes as rates recovered after an earlier short lived dip in rates. Rates steadied and showed signs of moving up on key routes.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A strong week for panamax tonnage in the Atlantic with July tonnage in short supply both north and south. A 75,000 tonner fixed from Amsterdam for a Murmansk/Israel coal run and agreed $11,250 daily for redelivery Gibraltar with still a lengthy list of July-early August cargoes from the US Gulf, Baltic, Black Sea and South America to be fixed for destinations within the Atlantic.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A much better week for the supras in the Atlantic with longer duration front-haul business helping to shorten tonnage lists. In the US Gulf, a nice 2015 built 63,300 dwt ultramax was booked for a trip to Singapore-Japan at a firm $18,000 daily.
